honest: Productive but scattered. Too many threads at once.
summary: Built AI course materials and technical interview slides while juggling engineering tasks. Got sidetracked by email triage dashboard prototype.
lessons: Bilingual materials work better than English-only for mixed audiences.
Ocean Gradient palette makes technical slides pop without distraction.
Mobile tap targets need explicit sizing — don't assume defaults.
Persistent artifacts beat one-off tools for daily workflows.
PDF to Word conversion requires style overrides for consistency.

items.title: SEEU_AI_COURSE
items.body: I was trying to generate comprehensive teaching materials for Day 1 of an AI Agent course.
decisions: Used bilingual Chinese/English with Chinese primary
Structured 5 modules covering agent fundamentals
Included code snippets and architecture descriptions
Focused on OpenAI Agents SDK for beginners
open: Need to verify demo code functionality
Should add troubleshooting tips for common errors
May need to adjust timing based on student feedback

items.title: Fetch yage newsletter
items.body: Fetch the latest newsletter from yage-ai.kit.com and save it to my SOP folder.
decisions: Ran the fetch script as specified
Script successfully discovered and downloaded new posts
Archived the new post in the newsletters/yage-ai/ directory

items.title: lets fix all
items.body: I was trying to create demo slides and a discussion-driving doc for a technical interview presentation.
decisions: Used Ocean Gradient color palette (navy, teal, cream, orange)
Created 14-slide PPTX with structured content sections
Built discussion doc with timing guide and Q&A prep
Fixed vertical alignment issues in text frames
Added visual elements to avoid text-only slides

items.title: Check files.zip for next task
items.body: I was trying to complete Sprint 2.1 by building the check-in UI and running engineering and design reviews.
decisions: Bumped sign-out button to h-10 for mobile tap targets
Removed redundant aria-label from HabitRow
Fixed dead fallback in timezone handling
Proceeded with Sprint 2.1 after reviews
open: Code review fixes ready to commit

items.title: New live artifact
items.body: I wanted to create a live artifact for daily email triage using Gmail and Calendar.
decisions: Focus on an email triage dashboard
Use Gmail for thread search and message reading
Integrate Calendar for meeting context
Make it a persistent sidebar artifact
open: What specific Gmail filters to apply?
How to prioritize emails (unread, starred, labels)?
Should it show upcoming meetings inline?

items.title: Generate Word version of outline
items.body: I was trying to generate a Word version of the final outline from the PDF.
decisions: Used docx-js to create the document
Set explicit page size to US Letter with 1" margins
Used Arial font for universal compatibility
Overrode built-in heading styles with custom formatting
Validated the document after creation
Prioritized bilingual AI course materials
Structured interview slides around discussion flow
Bumped mobile button height for accessibility
Chose Gmail/Calendar integration for email triage
Overrode Word heading styles for clean output
open_threads: Adjust course timing based on feedback
